the_signal.headline: Context engineering is now the production reliability multiplier
the_signal.body: N8n's deep dive on context engineering for LLMs surfaces the real problem: token budgets, context rot, and prompt design aren't separate concerns—they're a unified reliability architecture. GTM engineers shipping agents in production need context management frameworks before they scale, not after reliability breaks.

tool_drop.name: Rewisp
tool_drop.what: Ambient memory system for macOS that captures context without explicit logging.
tool_drop.why: If you're building sales/customer success workflows, persistent context without friction is the gap between agents that learn from interactions and those that restart cold every call.

thread_of_day.title: Agent Identity, Reliable Execution, and Intent Are Only Half-Way Solved
thread_of_day.takeaway: After analyzing 75 expected capabilities across n8n, Google, and Gumloop tooling, the industry still lacks standardized patterns for agent identity verification, deterministic execution, and intent routing—three foundational GTM problems.

quote.text: Why many production LLM systems use both [fine-tuning and RAG]. Fine-tuning vs. RAG isn't either/or—it's architectural layering.

whats_moving.context: As Alibaba pushes open-weight models with competitive inference, proprietary API costs for revenue systems become a margin conversation, not just a convenience one.
N8n's compliance automation content signals GTM orgs need audit trails and governance built into their AI workflows from day one, not retrofitted after incidents.
AI-powered incident workflows combining RAG + threat intelligence are moving from SOC-only to revenue-blocking GTM workflows (payment failures, API degradation, lead pipeline disruption).

act_on_this: Audit your agent's context window usage across a week of production calls—map where context rot happens (old customer data, outdated deal stage, stale intent signals) and build a context refresh policy before scaling to 10x volume.

numbers.stat: 75 capabilities expected from modern agent dev tools, yet still only partially solved
numbers.context: This fragmentation means GTM engineers are building reliability patterns from scratch instead of inheriting battle-tested frameworks—each new agent platform costs you 2-4 weeks of context/identity/execution hardening.
